title: Solitons with Fermion Number 1/2
canonical_scope: External source anchor for the domain-wall zero-mode argument reused in the topological-phases edge-mode family.

section_map.title: Main Result
section_map.summary: An isolated Dirac zero mode in a soliton background implies a topologically protected fermionic mode with fractionalized quantum numbers.

section_map.title: Isolated Zero Mode In A Soliton Background
section_map.summary: A Dirac operator in a topologically nontrivial background can support an isolated zero-energy solution whose wavefunction is localized by the soliton profile.

section_map.title: Fractional Fermion-Number Doublet
section_map.summary: Occupying or emptying the isolated zero mode produces a doublet whose fermion number differs by one and is centered around one half.

equation_map.label: soliton-dirac-zero-mode
equation_map.title: Dirac equation admitting an isolated zero-energy mode

equation_map.label: half-fermion-number-doublet
equation_map.title: Fractional fermion number from the isolated zero-mode occupancy
